title: String Methods Essentials 🔤
description: Transform and inspect strings with built-in methods.

slides.title: Strings Are Powerful! 💪
slides.content:
# String Methods

Strings aren't just text — they come with **dozens of built-in methods** for transformation and inspection!

**Case Methods:**
| Method | Result |
|--------|-------|
| `"hello".upper()` | `"HELLO"` |
| `"HELLO".lower()` | `"hello"` |
| `"hello world".title()` | `"Hello World"` |
| `"hello world".capitalize()` | `"Hello world"` |
| `"Hello".swapcase()` | `"hELLO"` |

All string methods return a **new string** — they never modify the original (strings are immutable!).

slides.title: Case Methods 🎬
slides.codeSnippet:
text = "hello, Python World!"

print(text.upper())       # HELLO, PYTHON WORLD!
print(text.lower())       # hello, python world!
print(text.title())       # Hello, Python World!
print(text.capitalize())  # Hello, python world!
print(text.swapcase())    # HELLO, pYTHON wORLD!

# Original is unchanged!
print("Original:", text)

quizData.question: What does "hello".upper() return?
quizData.explanation: .upper() converts all characters to uppercase: HELLO.

slides.title: Checking Strings 🔎
slides.content:
## Inspection Methods

These return `True` or `False`:

| Method | Checks if... |
|--------|-------------|
| `.isalpha()` | All letters (no numbers/spaces) |
| `.isdigit()` | All digits |
| `.isalnum()` | All letters OR digits |
| `.isspace()` | All whitespace |
| `.startswith(x)` | Starts with x |
| `.endswith(x)` | Ends with x |

```
"hello".isalpha()     # True
"12345".isdigit()     # True
"hello123".isalnum()  # True
"  ".isspace()        # True
```

slides.title: Stripping Whitespace 🧹
slides.content:
## Clean Up Your Strings!

`strip()` removes **leading and trailing whitespace** (spaces, tabs, newlines):

```
text = "   Hello World   "
print(text.strip())   # "Hello World"
print(text.lstrip())  # "Hello World   " (left only)
print(text.rstrip())  # "   Hello World" (right only)
```

You can also strip specific characters:
```
print("###Hello###".strip("#"))  # "Hello"
```

💡 This is crucial for cleaning user input! People often accidentally add extra spaces.

quizData.question: Do string methods modify the original string?

options.text: Yes, they change it in place
No, they return a new string

quizData.explanation: Strings are immutable. All methods return a NEW string; the original is never changed.
